headline: Duke Energy Cost Recovery Bid And 19,000 MW Plan Test Valuation
summary: Duke Energy (NYSE:DUK) has filed to recover extraordinary fuel and purchased power costs tied to this winter's extreme cold and record electricity demand. The company is also outlining plans for more than 19,000 MW of new generation capacity to support future energy demand and grid reliability. Duke Energy, trading at $128.03, is bringing two key issues to the forefront for investors: near term cost recovery and long term capacity expansion. The shares are up 9.0% year to date and 55.7%...

headline: This Is Why BMO Capital Raised Duke Energy (DUK)’s Price Target
summary: Duke Energy Corporation (NYSE:DUK) is one of the top utility stocks to buy now. On April 13, analysts at BMO Capital reiterated an Outperform rating on Duke Energy Corporation (NYSE:DUK) and increased the price target to $143 from $136. The research firm reiterates its expectation of a limited set of updates in the upcoming May […]

headline: NextEra Energy vs. Duke Energy: Which Utility Stock Has Better Upside?
summary: NEE has an edge over DUK, with stronger earnings estimate momentum, higher ROE, lower debt use and better one-year share gains.
